inline_comments.message: Referrencing a context variable in same set node won't work - as SetNodeExecutor sets params in random order.
source and outputPath seems same - so what is really being replaced ?
if `tmp.deleteSliceProfileEntry.url` is anyway being overriden here in this execute bock then what is significance of above switch case ?

Those are not same nodes. The intermediate calculation results are assigned to a temp variable with '1' at the end and at the second step, the actual value is derived.
For eg. tmp.nearRTRIC.uLThptPerSlice1 is an intermediate step result and tmp.nearRTRIC.uLThptPerSlice is the final result.

The switch case identifies the branch either to work with or without RAN Simulator integration and only the idNearRTRIC alone replaced. At this step, sliceProfileId is replaced which is common to both the branches.

/restconf/config/network-topology:network-topology/topology/topology-netconf/node/{mountName}/yang-ext:mount/ran-network:ran-network/NearRTRIC/{idNearRTRIC}/attributes/sliceProfilesList/{sliceProfileId}

We have also done this in the past, However, what Kapil is saying that node value assignments in randomly done, I have split this node into 2 nodes to address Kapil's observation. Thank you Kapil! I am submitting a patch for the same, and I would appreciate if you please accept and merge. Thank you.

Hi Kapil - we have successfully done this previously...Essentially, as Ahila is stating, URL used for RESTCONF transaction is different in two scenarios: 1) without RAN simulator (essentially executing against the local YANG tree for testing); and 2) with RAN simulator when RESTCONF transactions are actually executed against Netconf mounted RAN simulator deployed for managed elements in O-RAN network. We have successfully done this against Netconf mounted network/managed elements in the past. I would appreciate if you please concur and accept for merge.
